debug_info.goal:
ι : Type u_1
Ω : Type u_2
E : Type u_3
A : Finset ι
m : ℕ
inst✝³ : MeasurableSpace Ω
μ : Measure Ω
inst✝² : IsFiniteMeasure μ
mE : MeasurableSpace E
inst✝¹ : NormedAddCommGroup E
inst✝ : InnerProductSpace ℝ E
X : ι → Ω → E
iIndepFun_X : iIndepFun X μ
integral_X : ∀ (i : ι), ∫ (ω : Ω), X i ω ∂μ = 0
memLp_X : ∀ i ∈ A, MemLp (X i) (2 * ↑m) μ
X₁ : ι → Ω × Ω → E := ⋯
X₂ : ι → Ω × Ω → E := ⋯
X' : ι → Ω × Ω → E := ⋯
this : DecidableEq ι
⊢ ∀ i ∈ A, MemLp (X' i) (2 * ↑m) (μ.prod μ)

failed_attempts:
(fun i hi ↦
        by
          have h₁ : ∀ i ∈ A, MemLp (X' i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
            intro i hi
            have h₂ : MemLp (X₁ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
              have h₃ : MemLp (X₁ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
                -- Prove that X₁ i is in L^{2m}
                have h₄ : X₁ i = (X i) ∘ (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.1) := rfl
                rw [h₄]
                have h₅ : Measurable (X i : Ω → E) := by
                  exact (iIndepFun_X.tupleMeasurable (singleton i)).1
                have h₆ : MemLp (X i) (2 * m) μ := memLp_X i hi
                have h₇ : (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.1) = (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.1) := rfl
                have h₈ : Integrable (fun p : Ω × Ω => ‖(X i) p.1‖ ^ (2 * m)) (μ.prod μ) := by
                  -- Use the fact that X_i is in L^{2m} to show that the integral of ‖X_i‖^{2m} is finite
                  have h₉ : Integrable (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ (2 * m)) μ := by
                    exact (memLp_X i hi).integrable.norm_pow _
                  have h₁₀ : Integrable (fun p : Ω × Ω => ‖(X i) p.1‖ ^ (2 * m)) (μ.prod μ) := by
                    apply integrable_fst.mul_right _ _ <;>
                      simp_all [Integrable.mul_left, integrable_const, pow_nonneg]
                  exact h₁₀
                -- Use the integrability to conclude that (X i) ∘ (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.1) is in L^{2m}
                exact ⟨h₅.comp_measurable.s q (by simp_all [ProdType.fst'_measurable]) h₈, h₈⟩
              exact h₃
            have h₃ : MemLp (X₂ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
              -- Prove that X₂ i is in L^{2m}
              have h₄ : X₂ i = (X i) ∘ (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.2) := rfl
              rw [h₄]
              have h₅ : Measurable (X i : Ω → E) := by
                exact (iIndepFun_X.tupleMeasurable (singleton i)).1
              have h₆ : MemLp (X i) (2 * m) μ := memLp_X i hi
              have h₇ : (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.2) = (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.2) := rfl
              have h₈ : Integrable (fun p : Ω × Ω => ‖(X i) p.2‖ ^ (2 * m)) (μ.prod μ) := by
                -- Use the fact that X_i is in L^{2m} to show that the integral of ‖X_i‖^{2m} is finite
                have h₉ : Integrable (fun ω : Ω => ‖X i ω‖ ^ (2 * m)) μ := by
                  exact (memLp_X i hi).integrable.norm_pow _
                have h₁₀ : Integrable (fun p : Ω × Ω => ‖(X i) p.2‖ ^ (2 * m)) (μ.prod μ) := by
                  apply integrable_snd.mul_right _ _ <;>
                    simp_all [Integrable.mul_left, integrable_const, pow_nonneg]
                exact h₁₀
              -- Use the integrability to conclude that (X i) ∘ (fun p : Ω × Ω => p.2) is in L^{2m}
              exact ⟨h₅.comp_measurable.s q (by simp_all [ProdType.snd'_measurable]) h₈, h₈⟩
            -- Use the fact that X₁ i and X₂ i are in L^{2m} to show that X₁ i - X₂ i is in L^{2m}
            have h₄ : MemLp (X₁ i - X₂ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
              -- Use the Minkowski inequality to show that the difference is in L^{2m}
              -- since both X₁ i and X₂ i are in L^{2m}
              exact h₂.sub h₃
            simpa [X₁, X₂, X'] using h₄
          exact h₁ i hi
        )

h₁
  <;>
  simp_all [X₁, X₂, X']
  <;>
  try norm_num
  <;>
  try linarith

(2 * m : ℝ≥0) (μ.prod μ) :=
    h₂.sub h₃
  exact h₄

(fun i hi ↦
        let Y : ι → (Ω × Ω) → E := fun j x => (X j ∘ Prod.snd) x
        let h2 : (fun x : Ω × Ω => (X' i) x) = (fun x : Ω × Ω => (X i ∘ Prod.fst) x - (X i ∘ Prod.snd) x) := rfl
        have h3 : ∫ x : Ω × Ω, ‖(X' i) x‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ < ∞ :=
          by
            have h4 : ∫ x : Ω × Ω, ‖(X' i) x‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ =
                ∫ x : Ω × Ω, ‖(X i ∘ Prod.fst) x - (X i ∘ Prod.snd) x‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ := by simp [h2]
            rw [h4]
            have h5 : ∫ x : Ω × Ω, ‖(X i ∘ Prod.fst) x - (X i ∘ Prod.snd) x‖ ^ (2 * m) ∂μ.prod μ < ∞ := by
              have h6 : (memLp_X i hi)
              sorry
            exact h5
          h3
      )

(by
        -- Prove that each X' i is in L^(2 * m)(μ.prod μ)
        intro i hi
        have h₁ : MemLp (X i) (2 * m) μ := memLp_X i hi
        have h₂ : MemLp (X₁ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
          -- X₁ i is just X i composed with the projection, so it's in L^(2 * m)
          have h₂₁ : X₁ i = (X i) ∘ Prod.fst := rfl
          rw [h₂₁]
          exact (memLp_fst.2 (LpMap.fst (2 * m) _))
            <;> (try simp_all [h₁]) <;>
              (try simp_all [LpMap.fst]) <;>
                (try simp_all [LpMap.snd]) <;>
                  (try simp_all [iIndepFun_X])
        have h₃ : MemLp (X₂ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := by
          -- Similarly for X₂ i
          have h₃₁ : X₂ i = (X i) ∘ Prod.snd := rfl
          rw [h₃₁]
          exact (memLp_snd.2 (LpMap.snd (2 * m) _))
            <;> (try simp_all [h₁]) <;>
              (try simp_all [LpMap.fst]) <;>
                (try simp_all [LpMap.snd]) <;>
                  (try simp_all [iIndepFun_X])
        -- Since X₁ i and X₂ i are in L^(2 * m), their difference X' i is as well
        have h₄ : X' i = X₁ i - X₂ i := rfl
        rw [h₄]
        -- Use the fact that the difference of two Lp functions is in Lp
        have h₅ : MemLp (X₁ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := h₂
        have h₆ : MemLp (X₂ i) (2 * m) (μ.prod μ) := h₃
        exact h₅.sub h₆
      )
    -- ... (remaining proof steps)
    <;> simp_all [X₁, X₂, X', marcinkiewiczZygmundConst, marcinkiewiczZygmundSymmConst]
    <;> norm_num
    <;> linarith
  <;> simp_all [X₁, X₂, X', marcinkiewiczZygmundConst, marcinkiewiczZygmundSymmConst]
  <;> norm_num
  <;> linarith

(by
        intro i hi
        have h₁ : MemLp (X i) (2 * m) μ := memLp_X i hi
        sorry
      )
